title: Ex-NOAA employees re-create a valuable climate data site shut down by Trump
description: Former NOAA staffers have launched a new website that provides climate information. It replaces a government site that was shut down when the Trump administration took office.

title: Problem Solvers Caucus co-chairs say they'll make sure housing bill becomes law
description: Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick, R-Penn., and Rep. Tom Suozzi, D-N.Y., tell NPR's Michel Martin they want to force into law a housing bill President Trump has refused to sign.

title: UN agency pauses evacuation of ships through the Strait of Hormuz after attack on vessel
Inside Trump’s plans for retribution and other lesser-known allegations in ‘Regime Change’
description: A new book from two Washington reporters reveals what took place inside the White House after President Donald Trump’s return to power.

title: Rubio’s new job? Selling Trump’s Iran deal to a worried Gulf.
description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio met with Arab Gulf leaders to reassure them that U.S. isn’t cutting them out of negotiations with Tehran.

title: Migrants not entitled to apply for asylum while standing on Mexico side of border, Supreme Court rules
description: The 6-3 decision clears the way for the Trump administration to resume allowing federal agents at the border to turn back asylum seekers before they enter.

title: Trump works to woo farmers in aftermath of Iran war
description: President Trump's proposal to have Iran use their unfrozen funds to buy American agriculture products says a lot about where he's worried about lost political support.

title: Democratic socialists cemented power in New York. Next, the rest of the country?
description: The Democratic Socialists of America won with support outside their usual base. Winning outside New York may be a different challenge.

title: Judge blocks Postal Service proposal to restrict mail-in voting under Trump's order
description: A judge has blocked the U.S. Postal Service's proposals responding to President Trump's order, including not delivering ballots in states that don't turn over voter lists to the federal government.

title: Supreme Court backs Monsanto in its fight against liability from popular weed killer
description: The central issue in the Roundup case, filed by Missouri resident John Durnell, was who decides what should appear on a pesticide or insecticide label and whether a federal law overrides state claims.

title: A federal judge in Boston blocks key parts of Trump's order to limit voting by mail
description: A federal judge in Boston has blocked parts of President Trump's executive order to limit voting by mail. The Trump administration is expected to appeal the ruling.

title: Trump urges GOP to prioritize election bill as his Iran war handling draws scrutiny
description: President Trump visited Capitol Hill Wednesday and faced off with Senate Republicans upset about his handling of the Iran War, after scrapping plans to sign a bipartisan housing affordability bill.

title: Political strategist on the growing tensions between Trump and Senate Republicans
description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with political strategist Alex Conant about President Trump's tense meeting with Republican senators on Capitol Hill.

title: Trump is pushing to institutionalize homeless people. That may include veterans
description: A Trump executive order pushes involuntary treatment for homeless people; the VA denies that would include homeless vets.

title: White House seeks additional $88 billion from Congress for Iran war, other priorities
description: The request mostly includes money for the Pentagon, with $67 billion going toward replenishing the military’s stocks of munitions and the cost of sending so many forces to the Middle East.

title: Trump abruptly cancels signing of bipartisan bill on affordable housing
description: The president posted on social media that the signing was off until the Senate passes his election integrity bill. It is unclear whether he intends to kill the housing bill.

title: Socialist election wins spawn wider identity questions for Democrats
description: Tuesday’s primary victories for a slate of leftist candidates endorsed by New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani foretell bigger battles for a Democratic Party trying to win back power nationally.

title: Democratic socialist won N.Y. primary despite scrutiny over inflammatory posts
description: Darializa Avila Chevalier, a first-time candidate who unseated a House Democrat, has spent years advocating for Palestinians.

title: Hegseth thwarted internal efforts to extend key Army general’s career
description: Gen. Christopher Donahue, seen as a top warfighter, is the latest apparent casualty in a purge of senior military leaders by the Trump administration.

title: Democratic socialists are winning key races in cities. What that label means.
description: Zohran Mamdani won the New York mayor contest last year, and two candidates he and the Democratic Socialists of America backed have now won U.S. House primaries.

title: The 4 GOP senators who broke ranks and voted to block Trump from resuming Iran war
description: The president seethed at Susan Collins, Bill Cassidy, Lisa Murkowski and Rand Paul after the war powers resolution passed, saying: “I will get it done, one way or the other.”
